What's going on? All right, so process improvement at the end of every sale. You want to make sure that you schedule two appointments, not one. And this is something that came hot off the presses from R&D and something that we've been testing and seems to be working out awesome. All right, and so make sure that you do this. Obviously the first one you make sure you schedule is nutrition orientation. You don't want to re-reach out to the people after you just close the sale. Make sure you schedule nutrition orientation at the end of that sale so they know when they're coming back. All right, the next one is the abandon-hope meeting. All right, so it's the abandon-hope sale and that's typically 21 days later. Now to them, that's not called the abandon-hope sale. To them it's called the mandatory halfway check-in which is point C here right at the bottom. All right, now the reason we do this is because when you book it in advance, it increases the show up rate because they perceive it as more important because you did it a long time in advance. Whereas if you try and do it a couple days beforehand or halfway through week three, they're going to be like, whatever. But if you booked it out, at that point they know that they associate it with something they purchased. All right, and that's something that we're finding. So the show up rates on those are almost 100% now rather than having to work them and try and get them to show which is huge, which maximizes the amount of people you can sell on the backside. All right, so make sure that you schedule both appointments at the halfway point. Okay, so that's after week three. So they've done week one, week two, week three. And during that, right after they've had their halfway point weigh-in is when you want to meet with them. And it's like, that's our mandatory halfway point check-in and that's just so that we can make any adjustments necessary to maximize the second half of the race and get you to your goal. Right? That makes sense. You're just going to set the stage, provide more value even, and they'll associate it with the purchase. And that is how you'll get more people to shop.
